mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in industrial production signals expanding economic output, boosting corporate earnings expectations and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns. Rising INDPRO reflects real economic momentum that underpins equity valuations.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or monthly INDPRO growth shows zero predictive correlation with next-20-day SPX returns (Pearson |r| < 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate falls below 3.5%, the labor market becomes excessively tight, fueling wage inflation fears and prompting preemptive monetary tightening, which depresses equity valuations and leads to negative forward returns.
failure_condition: Over the next 5 years, SPX 20-day returns are not significantly lower in UNRATE < 3.5% periods vs. other periods (t-test p > 0.05), or the negative signal produces more than 45% positive instances.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales growth persistently outpacing industrial production growth signals demand-side overheating relative to supply capacity, creating inflationary pressures that erode real earnings and compress valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Rank IC of the RSAFS/INDPRO growth gap against 60-day forward returns is below 0.05 over any 3-year window, or the signal fails to predict negative returns in > 55% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Declining housing starts during the late-cycle expansion phase signal fading economic momentum and rising vulnerability to downturn, as residential investment typically leads the cycle. Falling HOUST indicates reduced consumer confidence and weaker future demand, dragging equity prices lower.
failure_condition: In post-1990 US business cycles, the negative relationship between 3-month HOUST decline and 60-day SPX return fails to produce significant negative returns (mean return > 0) or the signal loses predictive power after controlling for other macro factors (partial correlation not significant at 5%).

mechanism_narrative: Rising consumer price inflation directly erodes the real value of future corporate cash flows and increases the probability of central bank tightening, weighing on equity valuations and leading to negative short-term S&P 500 returns within a week.
failure_condition: Monthly CPI surprise (actual vs. median forecast) shows no significant negative correlation with next 5-day SPX returns over a 10-year period (Spearman rho > -0.05, p > 0.10).

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly payroll gains above 200,000 signal a robust labor market and accelerating economic growth, boosting corporate earnings and consumer spending, which drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the following month.
failure_condition: Average 20-day SPX return following payroll reports with gains > 200k is not significantly different from zero (t-test p > 0.10) or is negative over any consecutive 5-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When both the unemployment rate is very low (tight labor market) and the federal funds rate is elevated (restrictive monetary policy), the economy faces a policy constraint that is unusually unfavorable for equities, as it combines high wage pressure with high discount rates, compressing valuations and producing negative forward returns.
failure_condition: In instances where the trigger condition holds, the distribution of 20-day SPX returns is not significantly negative (median return ≤ 0) over the full sample, or the isolated interaction term in a regression is not significant at the 5% level.

mechanism_narrative: In the early recovery phase following a recession, strong GDP growth reflects pent-up demand and receding uncertainty, driving a cyclical upswing in corporate profits and boosting S&P 500 returns. The signal is strongest when the economy is emerging from a trough.
failure_condition: In quarters classified as early recovery (first two quarters after a NBER trough), the correlation between GDPC1 growth and subsequent 20-day SPX return is negative or insignificant (p > 0.10), or the average excess return over Treasuries during those periods is not positive.
